notes: 200+ IBA technology sponsors as of May 2002.
IBA uses up to 12 wires at 2.5 Gb/s each; standard configs: 1x/4x/12x for up to 30 Gbps.
PCI at 64-bit 66 MHz = 532 MB/s aggregate; shared by all devices.
Each IBA link minimum 500 MB/s vs PCI shared 500 MB/s; IBA scales to 6 GB/s per server.

metric_name: IBA TCO reduction for database clusters
notes: Early adopters expect ~50% TCO reduction for database clusters; I/O management cost savings ~70%.
Early adopters cite ~70% lower I/O management costs with InfiniBand adoption.
Early adopters observe near-linear scaling of TP/DB apps due to reduced clustering overhead.

metric_name: TCP/IP processing freed by IBA router
notes: IBA router terminates TCP/IP traffic; frees database server capacity; stretches software licenses.
Aberdeen Group sees all major computer suppliers offering IBA-enabled blades in 2003.
Aberdeen: IBA can begin enterprise production in 2003 with careful planning.

metric_value: IBA will replace PCI-X as next-generation I/O interconnect
notes: Aberdeen predicts planners should evaluate how IBA will augment then replace existing proprietary I/O.

metric_value: dense computing and consolidation via IBA
notes: IBA enables very dense blade server racks: single IBA cable per blade replaces SCSI/FC/Ethernet cabling.
Initial HCAs from Adaptec, Agilent, Emulex, IBM, QLogic, Voltaire in late 2002/early 2003.
Compaq/HP, Dell, IBM, Unisys plan to deliver new enterprise servers with HCA on system board in 2003.
SCSI ubiquitous in IT world; majority of storage apps use parallel SCSI; IBA TCA bridges SCSI to IBA.

metric_value: high cost and complexity limits to large enterprise data centers
notes: FC limited to large enterprise due to high cost vs. SCSI; IBA could route FC traffic via bridge devices.
Aberdeen expects TOE uses for IBA in data center in 2004.

metric_value: complementary: 3GIO replaces PCI; IBA handles I/O connectivity beyond motherboard
notes: 3GIO (PCI-E predecessor) is PCI replacement for board-level; IBA extends external I/O; they coexist.
IT planners needing scalable architecture should evaluate IBA in 2002 and move to phased deployment in 2003.

metric_value: emerged as preferred architecture for storage networking per Aberdeen research
notes: Aberdeen Group research: IBA fabric topology emerged as preferred architecture for storage networking.
